mnemonics: H.I.P.P.O. Cages - Hospitals, Industries, Practices, Products, Others creating Contaminated, Infectious, and General Biomedical waste
keyPoints: Major sources include hospitals, clinics, and healthcare facilities
Minor sources include laboratories, research centers, and home healthcare
Categories include infectious, pathological, sharps, chemical, pharmaceutical, and genotoxic waste
commonMistakes: Assuming only hospitals generate biomedical waste (labs, homes do too)
Believing all biomedical waste is equally hazardous (it has different risk categories)
Confusing biomedical waste with general municipal waste
